title: Weight change concern
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports avoiding diagnostic overshadowing, asking permission before weight discussion or measurements, and recording height, weight, BMI, waist or waist-to-height context and weight-related health risk.
assessment.rationale: Relevant to permission and clinician-entered measurements in possible weight gain without computing or diagnosing obesity.

source.title: Overweight and obesity management
source.population: Adults, children, and young people with overweight, obesity, or central adiposity, subject to section-specific age criteria.
source.setting: Identification, sensitive discussion, assessment, behavioural intervention, referral, and follow-up.
source.exclusions: Exact for selected permission-sensitive adult weight discussion, measurements, context, previous attempts, goals, clinician-discussed intervention or referral, and follow-up fields. It does not support automatic advice, medicine selection, dosing, or UAE referral pathways.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.9.1–1.9.17 — permission, measurements, and adult assessment

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports avoiding diagnostic overshadowing, asking permission before weight discussion or measurements, and recording height, weight, BMI, waist or waist-to-height context and weight-related health risk.

assessment.text: Supports comorbidity, social or mental-health context, diet or activity goals, previous attempts, experiences, barriers, preferences, clinician-agreed intervention, and preference-sensitive referral documentation.
assessment.rationale: Relevant only to clinician-completed context and discussion, not to generating a plan.

source.title: Overweight and obesity management
source.population: Adults, children, and young people with overweight, obesity, or central adiposity, subject to section-specific age criteria.
source.setting: Identification, sensitive discussion, assessment, behavioural intervention, referral, and follow-up.
source.exclusions: Exact for selected permission-sensitive adult weight discussion, measurements, context, previous attempts, goals, clinician-discussed intervention or referral, and follow-up fields. It does not support automatic advice, medicine selection, dosing, or UAE referral pathways.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.11.1–1.11.14 — results, context, intervention discussion, and referral

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports comorbidity, social or mental-health context, diet or activity goals, previous attempts, experiences, barriers, preferences, clinician-agreed intervention, and preference-sensitive referral documentation.

assessment.text: Supports documenting unintentional weight loss, appetite, taste, swallowing, bowel habit, prolonged illness, BMI, percentage weight loss, reduced intake and future intake risk when actually assessed.
assessment.rationale: Relevant to entered unintentional loss, intake, appetite, swallowing and illness context without calculating malnutrition.

source.title: Nutrition support for adults: oral nutrition support, enteral tube feeding and parenteral nutrition — Recommendations
source.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE nutrition, dietetics, consent, capacity, and referral adaptation
source.population: Adults in hospital and community settings with clinical concern for malnutrition or impaired intake.
source.setting: General practice, community, hospital, nutrition, and multidisciplinary assessment.
source.exclusions: Exact for documenting unintentional weight loss, poor appetite, altered taste, impaired swallowing, altered bowel habit, reduced intake, BMI and percentage weight loss when measured. It does not calculate risk or generate nutrition support.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.6 — screening, clinical concern, weight loss, intake, and swallowing

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting unintentional weight loss, appetite, taste, swallowing, bowel habit, prolonged illness, BMI, percentage weight loss, reduced intake and future intake risk when actually assessed.
